Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a lab document, such as SOPs, calibration records, and training materials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

fileUrl.description: URL or path to the document file.

uploadDate.description: Date and time when the document was uploaded.

category.description: Category or type of the document (e.g., SOP, Calibration Record).

Sample.title: Sample
Sample.description: Represents a lab sample with its registration details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Sample entity.

sampleId.description: Identifier assigned to the sample by the lab.

registrationDate.description: Date and time when the sample was registered.

storageLocation.description: Location where the sample is stored.

description.description: Optional description or notes about the sample.

Test.title: Test
Test.description: Represents a test assigned to a sample.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Test entity.

testName.description: Name of the test being performed.

assignedDate.description: Date when the test was assigned.

status.description: Current status of the test (e.g., 'Pending', 'In Progress', 'Completed').

reportId.description: Reference to Report. (Relationship: Report 1:1 Test).  ID of the report associated with this test, null if no report has been generated yet.

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Represents a test report generated for a sample.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Report entity.

generationDate.description: Date and time when the report was generated.

deviations.description: Any deviations observed during the test and their descriptions.

referenceLink.description: Link to any reference information used during the test (e.g., from online sources).

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client who submits samples for testing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

contactEmail.description: Email address of the client contact person.

contactPhone.description: Phone number of the client contact person.

definition.description: Stores documents (SOPs, records) owned by a specific user. Access is restricted to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the document.

definition.description: Stores client information. Accessible to lab administrators and client users with appropriate permissions.
Stores sample registration information. Accessible to lab administrators and the owning client.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The ID of the client who owns the sample.

definition.description: Stores test assignments for a given sample. Accessible to lab administrators and the owning client.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The ID of the client who owns the sample.

definition.description: Stores test reports. Accessible to lab administrators and the owning client.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The ID of the client who owns the sample.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the LabTrack application's requirements for document management, sample registration, test assignment, and report generation. The structure prioritizes authorization independence by avoiding hierarchical `get()` calls in security rules. This is achieved by leveraging path-based ownership for user-specific data and membership maps for collaborative data, ensuring atomic operations and simplified debugging.

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization):** Access control for samples, tests, and reports is implicitly linked to client ownership. Client ID is stored with samples to avoid rules requiring `get()` calls to other collections. Tests inherit the sample's client ID. Reports inherit the test's client ID.

**Structural Segregation:** Each collection houses data with a homogeneous security posture. User-specific documents are stored under `/users/{userId}/documents/{documentId}` to apply simple ownership-based rules.

**Access Modeling:** Path-based ownership is used for user documents. The `/clients/{clientId}/samples/{sampleId}` structure establishes clear ownership. Tests associated with samples reside under `/clients/{clientId}/samples/{sampleId}/tests/{testId}`, Reports associated with tests reside under `/clients/{clientId}/samples/{sampleId}/tests/{testId}/reports/{reportId}`.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**  The data structure allows secure `list` operations by clearly defining ownership and access rights at each level.  Path-based rules can efficiently filter collections based on the requesting user's ID or client ID, without needing complex filtering logic within the rules themselves.
